pr.title: All Ness functions complete and matching
pr.author: VetriTheRetri

summary: Completed the remaining Ness decompilation work by replacing asm/melee/ft/chara/ftNess/ftNess_8.s with a new C translation, adding prototypes to ftNess.h, moving/adding Ness action-state data in asm/melee/ft/chara/ftNess/ftNess_data.s, and switching obj_files.mk from the old Ness asm object to the new C object plus data object. The translated code covers Ness PSI Magnet start, hold, turnaround, absorb, and end handlers/callbacks across grounded and aerial variants. The dump contains no PR body or review comments, so intent and review rationale are inferred only from the title and diff.
what_changed: Deleted the 2117-line asm stub file asm/melee/ft/chara/ftNess/ftNess_8.s.
Added src/melee/ft/chara/ftNess/ftNess_8.c with 1012 lines of C for the remaining Ness functions, primarily PSI Magnet handlers and callbacks.
Added 54 ftNess.h prototypes for the newly translated symbols, including func_80119E14, func_80119E90, lbl_8011A0E0 through func_8011B4E4, and the s32-returning func_8011AB10.
Updated obj_files.mk to build src/melee/ft/chara/ftNess/ftNess_8.c.o instead of asm/melee/ft/chara/ftNess/ftNess_8.s.o, and added asm/melee/ft/chara/ftNess/ftNess_data.s.o.
Added asm/melee/ft/chara/ftNess/ftNess_data.s containing the Ness action-state table/data beginning at lbl_803CC650, with function-pointer entries for the newly translated callbacks.

Added asm/melee/ft/chara/ftfalcon.s with 3619 lines of Captain Falcon assembly. The PR context does not explain why this file is included, and obj_files.mk excerpt does not show it being wired into the build.
smart_moves: Kept Ness action-state data in a separate assembly data object while translating behavior code to C, reducing the amount of data-layout work required for a matching code PR.

Preserved original address-based symbol names such as func_80119E14 and lbl_8011A2A8 while adding descriptive comments that identify each routine as a grounded/aerial PSI Magnet Start, Hold, Turnaround, Absorb, or End callback.
Used decomp.me scratch links per function in comments, creating traceability for matching work and future cleanup.
Modeled repeated grounded/aerial action-state transitions explicitly with Fighter_ActionStateChange_800693AC action IDs such as 0x16F, 0x174, 0x170, 0x175, 0x173, and 0x178.
Factored one repeated sound/timer pattern into inline void MagnetStateVarCalc(HSD_GObj* fighter_gobj), showing a small reuse tactic while preserving the surrounding match-sensitive code shape.
Retained match-oriented artifacts such as unused stack arrays, goto labels, temporary variable splits, and explicit casts where needed to reproduce assembly.
decomp_lessons: For fighter special-move chunks, converting the text section first while leaving action-state data in asm can be an effective way to land a large matching slice without forcing all data structs to be known.

Action-state callback tables need header prototypes for every referenced function before moving the object from asm to C; this PR added a contiguous block of ftNess.h declarations for the translated callbacks.
Grounded/aerial variants often share logic but differ in physics/collision helpers and action-state IDs; keeping both variants visibly parallel can make matching and later naming easier.

State variables at Fighter offsets x2340, x2348, x234C, and x2350 are central to this PSI Magnet implementation: cooldown-after-release, release/input gating, gravity delay, and periodic effect/sound timing are all represented through them in the translated code.

Effect handling for PSI Magnet includes efAsync_Spawn on fighter bone index 23, setting x2219_flag.bits.b0, and installing efLib_PauseAll/efLib_ResumeAll as hitlag callbacks; this pattern should be reused or recognized when decompiling related special effects.
When a function naturally matches with duplicated branches, as in func_8011AB10 and absorb animation handling, avoid over-refactoring unless the compiler still emits the same control flow.
No review discussion is available, so broader project preferences cannot be inferred beyond what the diff shows.
naming_conventions: Unidentified functions keep address-based names with func_XXXXXXXX for normal functions and lbl_XXXXXXXX for callback labels.
Function parameters commonly use HSD_GObj* fighter_gobj or HSD_GObj* arg0, and local Fighter pointers use names like fighter_data, fighter_data2, temp_fighter, temp_r31, and temp_r4.
Comments give semantic names even when symbols remain address-based, e.g. "Ness's grounded PSI Magnet Start Action State handler" and "Ness's aerial PSI Magnet Absorb Physics callback".

ftNessAttributes fields use descriptive x-offset-prefixed names such as x74_COOLDOWN_AFTER_RELEASE, x84_PSI_MAGNET_FRAMES_BEFORE_GRAVITY, x88_PSI_MAGNET_MOMENTUM_PRESERVATION, x8C_PSI_MAGNET_FALL_ACCELERATION, and x98_PSI_MAGNET_ABSORPTION_BUBBLE_BONE_ID.

Fighter fields are accessed through existing x-offset names such as x2D4_specialAttributes, x80_self_vel, x110_attr, xE0_ground_or_air, x2340_stateVar1, x2348_stateVar3, x234C_stateVar4, and x2350_stateVar5.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used dummy local arrays such as s32 filler[7] and s32 unused[...] to force stack layout and register allocation in matching C.
Used explicit integer casts on fields such as xE0_ground_or_air and x2340_stateVar1 to match comparison codegen.
Preserved goto-style control flow in aerial physics functions around gravity-delay handling to reproduce branch structure before common fall-speed updates.
Passed exact float constants 0.0f and 1.0f to Fighter_ActionStateChange_800693AC rather than hiding them behind abstractions.
Used literal action-state IDs and flags, including 0x0C4C5082, 0x16F, 0x170, 0x173, 0x174, 0x175, and 0x178, to match existing assembly call sites.
Kept null IASA callbacks as explicit empty functions returning immediately, matching original blr-only routines.
Moved raw action-state table bytes/function pointers to ftNess_data.s with .balign 8 and .4byte entries, avoiding premature C struct reconstruction.
Used an inline helper for MagnetStateVarCalc but left a similar sequence expanded in another absorb callback, suggesting inlining was applied only where it preserved the target output.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the PR context.
Because there is no human text excerpt, there is no direct evidence of reviewer-requested changes or naming feedback.
The unexplained addition of asm/melee/ft/chara/ftfalcon.s should not be treated as intentional review guidance; the slice provides no rationale.
searchable_terms: All Ness functions complete and matching
follow_up_queries: Check later PRs for semantic renames of Ness PSI Magnet functions and state variables.
Investigate whether asm/melee/ft/chara/ftfalcon.s was intentional, unused, or later removed/wired into obj_files.mk.
Look for subsequent conversion of ftNess_data.s action-state table into typed C data structures.
Search for cleanup of decomp.me scratch comments, unused arrays, and temp variable names in ftNess_8.c.
Compare this PSI Magnet state-variable pattern against other absorb/counter special moves for shared abstractions.
